summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew, bundling the server inside the app so users have a single install experience.
summary.outcome: A working Homebrew cask was created and published at nosman/homebrew-gossamer, with a GitHub Actions CI pipeline that builds macOS DMGs on tag push, auto-updates the cask with correct SHA256s, and documents the Gatekeeper workaround. Server startup via ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE was the final fix applied but not yet confirmed working.

repo: The project has two package.json files: root (server, gossamer-serve CLI) and app/ (Electron frontend). They were intentionally kept separate even after bundling.
server.ts uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT for Prisma binary location — bundled layout must preserve this path structure.
prisma was in devDependencies but is required at runtime by pushSchema — must be in production dependencies.
The app/ directory builds with electron-vite; the root directory is a plain Node.js/ESM server.
Native modules (better-sqlite3, node-pty) require rebuilding against Electron's ABI via @electron/rebuild before packaging.
The git remote is at github.com/nosman/gossamer on branch README for packaging work, main for README/workflow changes.

code.finding: utilityProcess.fork() has subtle limitations with dynamic ESM imports on macOS. Replaced with child_process.spawn using process.execPath + ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 env var for reliable Node-as-server spawning with matching ABI.
CJS wrapper needed to bridge utilityProcess.fork() (CJS-only) to the ESM server via dynamic import(). May be obsolete now that spawn approach is used.

YAML heredocs inside run: | blocks break YAML parsing due to indentation. Use sed substitution against a template file instead. Also requires permissions: contents: write on the job — GitHub Actions defaults to read-only.
electron-builder requires a repository field in package.json when run from a subdirectory — it cannot auto-detect the repo from a parent .git directory.
Cask template uses named placeholders (GOSSAMER_VERSION, GOSSAMER_ARM64_SHA256, GOSSAMER_X64_SHA256) substituted by sed in CI — avoids heredoc YAML issues.

workflow: electron-builder's --publish flag must be set to never when using a separate upload step (e.g. softprops/action-gh-release); otherwise it tries to publish itself and fails if not on a tag.
Fine-grained GitHub PATs for pushing to a tap repo need only Contents: Read and write on the target repo.
Homebrew Cask files must live in a Casks/ subdirectory of the tap repo.
DMG filenames are derived from app/package.json version, not the git tag — these must be kept in sync or the cask URLs will 404.
Unsigned/unnotarized macOS apps produce 'app is damaged' errors from Gatekeeper; xattr -cr /Applications/App.app removes the quarantine attribute as a workaround.
Homebrew caveats stanza is the standard place to surface post-install warnings to users.

friction: package-lock.json was out of sync with package.json after adding electron-builder, causing npm ci to fail in CI.
Work was split across two branches (main and README) plus the separate homebrew-gossamer repo, requiring worktrees and careful branch targeting for commits.
Network outage mid-session prevented pushing several commits, requiring deferred pushes.
Version mismatch between git tag (v1.0.7) and app/package.json version (1.0.0) caused DMG filenames to not match cask URLs, breaking brew install.
YAML heredoc syntax in the workflow file broke the CI pipeline and required a refactor to sed-based template substitution.
The workflow auto-updated homebrew-gossamer during the v1.0.9 run, creating conflicts when trying to push the caveat fix manually.
GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN defaults to read-only, requiring explicit permissions: contents: write to create releases.
open_items: Server startup via ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 approach was committed but not confirmed working in production.
app/resources/server-launcher.cjs may be dead code now that the spawn approach replaces utilityProcess.fork() — should be removed if confirmed.
No code signing or notarization — users must run xattr -cr workaround. Requires Apple Developer account ($99/yr) to fix properly.
The README branch containing packaging changes has not been merged into main — the project structure is split across branches.
Consolidating app/ and root into a single package was deferred as a meaningful refactor for future consideration.
The CI trigger was changed to branch push for testing and restored to tags — verify the tag-based trigger is working correctly end-to-end with a real release.
